name: Suspension Physics
description: A vehicle handling model where wheels or hover points compress against terrain, transferring contact information into ride height, grip, stability, and body roll.
design_notes: Suspension can make vehicles feel grounded without requiring full simulation accuracy. The design decision is how much road texture, jump landing, slope change, and impact instability the player should feel before the vehicle becomes tiring to control. Arcade games often exaggerate stability and response; simulation-leaning games expose more contact loss and body motion.
design_purpose: Create believable contact between vehicle bodies and uneven surfaces.
Give level designers terrain features that affect handling without manually scripting every bump.
Support readable differences between light, heavy, off-road, and high-performance vehicles.
player_fantasy: The player feels the machine bite into the road, absorb terrain, and recover from landings through skillful control.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.description: Vehicle archetype or instance owning the suspension profile.

parameters.name: wheel_contact_state
parameters.typical_range: per wheel: grounded, compression, normal, slip
parameters.description: Contact sample data cached for handling and effects.

parameters.name: suspension_stiffness
parameters.description: Compression resistance used by each wheel support.

parameters.name: damping_coefficient
parameters.description: Oscillation reduction applied after compression changes.

parameters.name: center_of_mass_offset
parameters.description: Body balance adjustment for rollover and cornering response.

parameters.name: ground_surface_id
parameters.description: Surface profile selected from the current contact material.

parameters.name: travel_limit_units
parameters.description: Maximum spring compression from rest height to full bump.

edge_cases: Only one wheel remains grounded on a curb or rock and the chassis receives a large roll impulse.
A vehicle lands from a jump with wheel contacts appearing over several physics steps instead of all at once.
Terrain triangles produce rapidly changing contact normals at road mesh seams.
Suspension samples continue after a vehicle is flipped, destroyed, or placed on a moving platform.
High-latency clients predict wheel contact differently from the server on rough terrain.
common_bugs: Wheel contact rays start inside the ground after large bumps, causing sudden launch impulses.
Damping is applied with frame delta instead of fixed-step delta and produces platform-dependent bounce.
Surface material changes update tire effects but not grip, making mud or ice look correct but drive like asphalt.
Center of mass edits are applied after rigidbody initialization and silently ignored by the engine.
Suspension debug data is not replayable, making terrain-specific handling reports hard to reproduce.
balancing_notes: Higher stiffness improves steering precision but can make rough terrain unreadable and punishing.
More damping reduces bounce after jumps but can make the vehicle feel heavy and unresponsive.
Lowering the center of mass improves stability, but too much stability removes the risk from jumps, curbs, and aggressive turns.
accessibility_notes: Offer camera shake and roll reduction for players sensitive to suspension-driven body motion.
Provide steering assist or stability assist presets when uneven terrain demands rapid correction.
Keep visual terrain cues readable so players can anticipate bumps before the handling changes.

implementation_notes.unity: Use WheelCollider for built-in setups or explicit raycasts per wheel in FixedUpdate. Store wheel_contact_state before applying motor and steering, set Rigidbody.centerOfMass from center_of_mass_offset during initialization, and draw contact normals and compression bars with Gizmos for tuning.
implementation_notes.godot: Sample suspension rays from VehicleBody3D or custom RayCast3D nodes during _physics_process. Keep compression, contact_normal, ground_surface_id, and slip estimates in vehicle state so camera, audio, particles, and traction all read the same contact result.
implementation_notes.unreal: Chaos Vehicles can expose wheel setup, suspension travel, spring rate, and damping per wheel. For custom vehicles, run line traces from wheel sockets, cache hit normals and surface types, and replicate compact wheel_contact_state only when remote prediction needs correction.
implementation_notes.web: In Three.js or a custom loop, separate rendered wheel animation from the fixed-step vehicle solve. Keep deterministic ray order, record contact samples in rollback snapshots, and avoid deriving grip from unsorted mesh triangle hits.

example_games.title: Forza Horizon 5
example_games.note: Uses suspension and surface response as part of vehicle handling.

example_games.title: Gran Turismo 7
example_games.note: Uses suspension behavior and tuning as part of car handling.

example_games.title: SnowRunner
example_games.note: Highlights suspension travel and terrain contact in off-road traversal.
